sector.name: Cement
sector.description: Cement makers scaling capacity and margins

fyPerformance: FY26 saw UltraTech cross 200 MnT cement capacity in India.
Q4 consolidated sales volume crossed 44 MnT, with UltraTech brand volume up 19% YoY.
India Cements and Kesoram brand migration was completed ahead of schedule.
EBITDA/ton excluding acquired assets stood at 1,296 in Q4FY26.
Balance sheet remained strong with consolidated net debt/EBITDA below 1x.
highlights: Green energy met around 43% of power needs.
India Cements EBITDA/ton improved to 497 in Q4.
Management expects integration benefits to reflect in FY27 P&L.
Kesoram assets are already operating near 1,000 EBITDA/ton.
Management is charting the next growth phase beyond 240 MnT capacity.
Dividend of 240 per share was recommended for FY26.
UltraTech Cement Ltd.quote: "We enter fiscal '27 in a stronger strategic position than at any point in our history."

fyPerformance: Q4 domestic cement volume grew 11% YoY to 10.56 MnT.
FY26 operating EBITDA grew 11% to 4,222 Cr, excluding one-time impact.
Union Cement delivered strong FY26 performance with revenue up 39%.
FY26 realization improved 3.6% YoY to 4,732/ton.
Q4 EBITDA/ton improved sequentially to 1,125.
highlights: Installed India cement capacity increased to 69.3 MnT.
Green electricity share stood at 61% in Q4FY26.
UAE cement mill of 2.5 MnT is scheduled by September 2026.
Kodla integrated project was commissioned during the quarter.
RMC network is expanding from 26 to 36 plants.
Meghalaya integrated cement project is under development.

fyPerformance: FY26 annual sales volume reached a record 73.7 MnT, up 16% YoY.
Normalized EBITDA grew 31% to 6,539 Cr, with EBITDA/ton at 887.
PAT grew 17% to 2,647 Cr while the company remained debt-free.
Trade sales volume grew 10% and premium cement formed 35% of trade sales.
Capacity increased to 109 MnT during FY26 through grinding and clinker additions.
highlights: Premium cement formed around 36% of Q4 trade sales.
Management targets 150-200/ton savings from raw material and green energy levers.
Penna and Sanghi utilization improvement remains a key FY27 focus.
Green power share improved to around 32% in Q4.
FY27 volume target is around 80 MnT.
Capacity is expected to reach around 119 MnT by FY27-end.
Ambuja Cements Ltd.quote: "FY27 focus firmly remains on streamlining operations and margin expansion."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 6% and volume grew 2%.
FY26 EBITDA reached a record 3,083 Cr, up 28% YoY.
Q4 EBITDA grew to 902 Cr, with EBITDA/ton at 1,023.
FY26 PAT jumped 65% YoY to 1,157 Cr.
Core cement ROCE improved from 9.9% to 12.1%.
highlights: Q4 cost/ton was the lowest quarterly cost in five years.
Renewable energy share improved to 47% in Q4.
Core cement ROCE improved by more than 200 bps to 12.1%.
Logistics cost declined 6% YoY to 1,064/ton in Q4.
Direct dispatch share reached a record 65%.
Belgaum, Pune and Kadapa projects are key capacity additions.
Dalmia Bharat Ltd.quote: "Maximizing ROCE from all our assets is a top agenda item."

fyPerformance: FY26 net sales grew 16% to 12,568 Cr.
FY26 EBITDA grew 18% to 2,318 Cr, with margin at 18.5%.
Q4 PAT rose 91% QoQ to 345 Cr.
Full-year PAT grew 21% to 1,033 Cr.
Q4 net sales grew 15% QoQ to 3,614 Cr.
highlights: Central India 6 MnT capacity expansion was commissioned.
Muddapur capacity increased by 1 MnT to 4.5 MnT.
Net debt/EBITDA stood at 1.45x.
Jaisalmer integrated project is expected in H1FY28.
Nathdwara wall putty plant is expected by September.
Management expects at least 2.5 MnT incremental FY27 volume.
JK Cement Ltd.quote: "We should be growing in double digits in FY27."

fyPerformance: FY26 volume grew around 4% despite operational challenges at a few plants.
Blended cement share improved to 88% from 82%.
Lead distance reduced to 337 km, supporting cost discipline.
Trade segment mix improved to 77% from 70%.
Mukutban volume improved to 27.7 lakh tons in FY26.
highlights: Mukutban volume improved to 27.7 lakh tons.
Peak net debt is expected around 4,000 Cr in the current capex cycle.
Cost impact from packaging and fuel is estimated at 150-175/ton.
Capacity is planned to expand from 21.5 MnT to 27.5 MnT by FY29.
Bikram coal block is expected to support cost savings.
Management remains cautious on forward guidance due to volatile inputs.
Birla Corporation Ltd.quote: "We are on a solid footing and sticking to our strategy."

fyPerformance: Management highlighted strong FY26 industry demand growth of around 7%.
Pricing recovered partly in Q4, but capacity additions restricted meaningful hikes.
The company is ramping Surat capacity and continuing work on Durg expansion.
Surat utilization has already crossed 60% and is expected to ramp further.
Management expects JK Lakshmi to grow ahead of industry in FY27.
highlights: Surat utilization has crossed 60% and may move above 70%.
Management expects to grow higher than the industry in FY27.
Fuel mix and clinker efficiency are key levers to protect margins.
FY27 industry demand growth is expected around 6%.
Durg project capex is around 3,000 Cr with 500 Cr already spent.
Energy and packaging inflation remain the main near-term margin risks.
JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd.quote: "We are relentlessly working on cost and margin improvement."

fyPerformance: FY26 was Nuvoco's strongest annual performance, with highest-ever volume and EBITDA.
Q4 marked the first 6 MnT quarter and record quarterly EBITDA of 590 Cr.
Premiumization improved 300 bps YoY to 43% in FY26.
FY26 EBITDA reached 1,881 Cr, the highest in company history.
Management highlighted infrastructure and housing demand as key FY27 tailwinds.
highlights: Vadraj Cement project remains on schedule.
East expansion will add 4 MnT grinding capacity in phases through FY28.
Management aims to keep debt around 2-2.5x EBITDA during investment phase.
Vadraj commissioning is planned in phases from Q3FY27 to Q1FY28.
Viramgam bulk cement terminal is targeted for FY28.
AFR share is targeted to rise from 10% to above 13% in FY27.
Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd.quote: "Our growth agenda is firmly on track."
